title: Yale Emerging Climate Leaders Fellowship
description: A five-month hybrid fellowship from Yale's Jackson School of Global Affairs for clean energy and climate leaders from developing countries in Asia, Africa, the Middle East, and Latin America/Caribbean. The program combines two week-long in-person sessions -- one at Yale in New Haven, Connecticut, and one in Paris, France -- bridged by bi-monthly remote "Learning Journeys." Yale covers round-trip airfare to both in-person sessions, meals, accommodation, airport transfers, and an allowance. Open to professionals (not just those working directly in climate/energy roles) across fields including finance, law, journalism, policy, entrepreneurship, and research, with a minimum of about 5 years of professional experience; no strict age limit. Applications for the 2027 cohort close July 30, 2026, 11:00 AM ET.
org: Yale Jackson School of Global Affairs
visaNote: Fellows travel to the U.S. and France for two short in-person sessions on a visitor visa; this is a leadership fellowship, not employment.
